note: Contiguous-storage rewrite of JunctionTreeCholesky (flat arenas + precomputed offsets + precomputed assembly map + reused scratch; no per-clique Vec alloc, no position() search in the hot path). SAME public API, SAME exact answer (4 exactness tests pass, example numbers byte-identical). Multifrontal solve 2.6-2.8x faster; measured speedup vs dense Gauss 5.9-255x -> 16.7-682x; flop/measured gap 5.0x -> 1.86x (large d). Closes E9's predicted ~5x headroom. Honest: dense baseline is Gaussian elim (~2x flops of pure Cholesky) so 682x partly reflects the slower baseline; a Cholesky baseline would show ~2x more residual.
